GT LEARN #767 🔀 Reorganize String MEDIUM Greedy Max-Heap Counting
s= exp: e.g. "ababac"
📝 Problem Statement
Given a string s, rearrange its characters so that no two adjacent characters are the same. Return any valid rearrangement, or "" if it's impossible.
🧠 Solving Approach
Greedy + Max-Heap:
1. Count character frequencies. If any char's count exceeds (n+1)//2, return "" — too many of one letter to separate.
2. Push (-count, char) tuples into a max-heap (Python heapq is min-heap, so negate).
3. Repeatedly pop the two most-frequent chars, append both to result, decrement counts, push survivors back.
4. If one char remains at end, append it.
🎯 Core Concepts
Feasibility check: if maxCount > (n+1)/2, impossible. Even alternating placement can't separate them.
Pop two at a time: ensures the two chars placed consecutively are different (heap top ≠ second-top).
Max-heap via negation: Python heapq is min-heap; push negative counts so largest count comes to top.
⏱ Time: O(n log k) k=26 💾 Space: O(k)
🧪 Test Cases (click to load)
SIMPLEsmall valid→ "aba"
"aab"
IMPOSSIBLE3 a's, 1 b — can\'t split→ ""
"aaab"
CLASSIC3 letters, varied→ "ababac"
"aaabbc"
TRICKYv dominates→ "vlvov"
"vvvlo"
EDGE3 a + 2 b→ "ababa"
"baaba"
Result: —
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization — Frequency, Max-Heap & Result
0/0
←→R 100%
